instance AggregatedContextData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated contextual data traces from all axes, abstracted for friction analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionSimulator
]

instance ModelParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Parameters estimated by white‑box and black‑box friction models"
]

instance PerformanceMetricsData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Derived performance metrics (energy consumption, wear estimation, trajectory error) produced by simulation"
]

// C2: Acting components (first axis example)
instance Axis1Actuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent‑magnet synchronous motor coupled with a cycloidal gear transmission on axis 1, providing closed‑loop position control with torque limits and motion‑range guards"
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLArchitectureModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML model (internal block diagram, state machines) describing requirements → functions → logical → physical hierarchy (RFLP methodology)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionSimulator
]

instance SimulinkWhiteBoxFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "White‑box physics‑based friction model implemented in Simulink for gearbox friction identification"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionSimulator
]

instance MachineLearningFrictionModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Black‑box data‑driven friction model (machine‑learning) learning nonlinear relationships from sensor streams"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionSimulator
]

instance DataTransformationPipelineSysMLtoSimulink : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Transformation pipeline preserving traceability from SysML artifacts to Simulink models"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkFrictionSimulator
]

// C11: Enablers
instance MQTTSensorBroker : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT broker providing low‑latency, bi‑directional streaming of encoder data to the Digital Twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeJointMonitoringService
]

instance SimulinkFrictionSimulator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Simulink simulation environment executing white‑box and black‑box friction models for online identification and prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables OnlineFrictionIdentificationService
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionErrorPredictionService
]

instance SinumerikController : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl controller receiving feed‑forward control adjustments from the Twin"
    DTDFVocab:enables FeedForwardControlAdjustmentService
]

instance WebDashboardVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Web‑based dashboard (JavaScript visualisation libraries) aggregating live sensor streams, model parameters and performance metrics"
    DTDFVocab:enables IntegratedVisualizationDashboardService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeJointMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time monitoring of joint positions, motor currents and encoder data"
    DTDFVocab:provides JointPositionInsight
]

instance OnlineFrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Robust online identification of white‑box and black‑box gearbox friction models"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionModelIdentificationInsight
]

instance FrictionErrorPredictionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Prediction of friction‑induced errors and their propagation to end‑effector trajectories"
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionErrorPredictionInsight
]

instance FeedForwardControlAdjustmentService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Feed‑forward control adjustments that compensate identified friction to improve motion accuracy"
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAdjustmentAction
]

instance IntegratedVisualizationDashboardService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated visualization of sensor streams, model parameters and performance metrics through a unified dashboard"
    DTDFVocab:provides DashboardVisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance JointPositionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Live insight into joint angles, velocities and currents for operator awareness"
]

instance FrictionModelIdentificationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight showing identified friction model parameters and confidence scores"
]

instance FrictionErrorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight predicting trajectory deviations caused by friction under current operating conditions"
]

instance ControlAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator‑initiated action to apply feed‑forward torque offsets based on model predictions"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DashboardVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Aggregated visual representation of sensor data, model outputs and performance metrics"
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT lifecycle follows the MBSE development flow split into abstraction layers: requirements engineering, functional architecture modeling, logical architecture design, and product/technical layer implementation. Representation evolves from high‑level SysML requirement diagrams to detailed SysML internal block diagrams, ADL component‑connector models (e.g., ACME, AADL, MontiArc) and finally executable code in C++/Java or simulation models in Simulink. Transitions between stages are managed by traceability links defined in the reference model, and governance is provided by the conformance relation of concrete realizations to the digital‑shadow reference model."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The documents stress that the reliability of the Digital Twin depends on the correctness of its underlying models and data transformations. Validation and verification activities include traceability of model transformations (e.g., from SysML to simulation models) and verification of both white‑box Simulink models and black‑box machine‑learning models. Uncertainty sources are identified in the data‑driven identification process, and quality assurance requires systematic verification of model fidelity against the aggregated contextual data traces. The approach calls for explicit verification of model transformations to ensure that model fidelity is maintained throughout the development chain."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin conforms to several industry and modeling standards. Its core artefacts are specified with standardized modeling languages (SysML, ACME, AADL, MontiArc) and stored as UML class diagrams, ensuring interoperability. The reference model adopts a formal conformance relation that aligns concrete realizations with the standardised concepts of the model. In the manufacturing domain, the Twin implements the Asset Administration Shell (AAS) specification, providing a certified interface for data exchange and lifecycle management. Compliance with these standards is documented in the model’s metadata, supporting certification processes and cross‑vendor interoperability."
]
